title: Illinois Quantum And Microelectronics Park Facilitates Immediate Ecosystem Entry For Future Tenants
why:
- The Illinois Quantum and Microelectronics Park (IQMP) is launching the On-Ramp program, enabling future tenants to immediately integrate into the Illinois quantum ecosystem through temporary locations with lab space and specialized equipment.
- The initiative provides access to equipment such as cryostats, lasers, control electronics, optical tables, and a 124-mile fiber optic quantum loop while the permanent IQMP facilities are under development.
- Leading quantum companies participating in the On-Ramp program include IBM, Pasqal, Diraq, and Quantum Machines.
- Partner organizations such as mHUB, the UChicago Science Incubator, and the Discovery Partners Institute host temporary lab space, supporting prototyping and collaboration for quantum startups and industry leaders.
- Early quantum tenants benefiting from the incubator program include memQ, Quantum Machines, Bluefors, and stac12.
